title: Self-Worth Tied to External Validation or Achievement
description: When self-worth becomes heavily dependent on external achievements, approval, or roles, it can create chronic pressure and a fragile sense of identity. The person may struggle to feel valuable when they are not performing or being validated.
observation_language: It seems like your sense of worth is closely connected to how well you’re doing or how others see you.
There appears to be a pattern where rest or 'not achieving' brings up discomfort or guilt.
It looks like being 'enough' without external proof is something that feels difficult or unfamiliar.
underlying_fears: Fear of being worthless without achievement
Fear of rejection if not performing
